meta.description: AI or Not? Game Content Library

items.attribution: Photo by Johny Goerend on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a dramatic aurora borealis. The vivid green and purple colors look surreal, but auroras genuinely produce these colors when solar particles excite nitrogen and oxygen in the atmosphere. The uneven glow and natural noise in the dark sky are clues this is real.

items.attribution: Photo by Samuel Ferrara on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a mountain landscape. The dramatic scale and lighting can make mountain photos look AI-generated, but notice the natural irregularity in the rock textures and cloud formations — AI tends to smooth these out or repeat patterns.

items.attribution: Photo by Matt Hardy on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of an ocean wave. The translucent water and dynamic spray might look too perfect, but real waves captured at high shutter speeds freeze water in ways that look surreal. Look for the randomness in the droplets — AI struggles to make water spray truly random.

items.attribution: Photo by Boris Smokrovic on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a colorful bird. The vibrant plumage looks almost too vivid to be real, but many bird species have evolved intensely colorful feathers. Look at the fine detail in individual feathers — AI often makes feathers look painted or blurry at the edges.

items.attribution: Photo by Manja Vitolic on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a cat. Cats are a common AI subject, but real cat photos have natural imperfections — slightly asymmetric ears, individual whisker detail, and realistic eye reflections. AI cat images often have too-smooth fur or oddly shaped pupils.

items.attribution: Photo by Jeffrey Hamilton on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of jellyfish. Their translucent, glowing bodies look almost computer-generated, but jellyfish genuinely have this ethereal appearance. The key tell is the organic irregularity in the tentacles — each one slightly different, with natural tangles that AI tends to over-regularize.

items.attribution: Photo by Annie Spratt on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real macro photograph of a flower. Macro photography reveals details invisible to the naked eye, which can look surreal. The pollen grains, petal texture, and tiny imperfections are all natural. AI-generated flowers often have impossibly smooth petals and symmetric structures.

items.attribution: Photo by Brandon Morgan on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of lightning. The branching pattern of real lightning is fractal — it splits unpredictably. AI-generated lightning often has too-regular branching or glows in ways that don't match real electrical discharge. The grainy sky and uneven brightness here are natural.

items.attribution: Photo by Aaron Burden on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real macro photograph of frost crystals. The geometric patterns in ice look almost too perfect to be natural, but ice crystals genuinely form repeating hexagonal structures. Notice the slight variations and imperfections in each crystal — real physics produces near-symmetry, not perfection.

items.attribution: Photo by Benjamin Voros on Unsplash
items.explanation: This is a real photograph of a starry sky over mountains. Long-exposure photography captures far more stars than the naked eye can see, making the sky look surreal. The giveaway that it's real: natural sensor noise in the dark areas and slight star trailing from Earth's rotation.

items.attribution: AI-generated with Stable Diffusion (not copyrightable)
items.explanation: This AI-generated landscape looks photorealistic at first glance, but look closely at the water reflections — they don't match the objects above them. AI often gets reflections wrong because it doesn't understand physics, it just patterns. Also check where land meets water for blurry, smeared transitions. [CURATOR: replace URL with a stable-diffusion landscape image from Lexica or Wikimedia Commons]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Midjourney (subject to Midjourney ToS)
items.explanation: This AI-generated image of a cat has telltale signs: the whiskers may split or merge unnaturally, the fur has an overly smooth, painted quality, and the background blurs in a way that doesn't match real camera depth-of-field. Compare to real cat photo img-005 and look for the differences in fur texture. [CURATOR: replace URL with a Midjourney cat image from Lexica or similar stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with DALL-E 3 (subject to OpenAI ToS)
items.explanation: This AI-generated food image looks appetizing but has subtle issues. Check the plate edges — AI often warps circular objects into slightly oval shapes. Look at the utensils: forks may have the wrong number of tines, and knife blades may merge into the handle oddly. Text on any labels or menus in the background will likely be gibberish. [CURATOR: replace URL with a DALL-E food/still-life image from a stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Stable Diffusion XL (not copyrightable)
items.explanation: This AI-generated flower looks beautiful but too perfect. Real flowers have asymmetric petals, insect damage, pollen dust, and slight wilting. This image has impossibly uniform petals and a background bokeh that's too smooth and circular. Nature is messy — perfection is a clue that something is AI. [CURATOR: replace URL with SDXL flower image from Lexica or similar stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Midjourney v5 (subject to Midjourney ToS)
items.explanation: This AI-generated cityscape at night is very convincing. The hardest AI images to spot are scenes with lots of lights and bokeh, because imperfections hide in the visual complexity. Look for repeated building patterns, windows that don't align properly between floors, and signage with nonsense text. Zoom in on small details — that's where AI falls apart. [CURATOR: replace URL with Midjourney cityscape from Lexica or similar stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Flux (not copyrightable)
items.explanation: This AI-generated bird image is photorealistic but has issues in the fine details. Look at the feet and claws — AI often gets bird feet wrong, adding extra toes or fusing them together. Check the eye: real bird eyes have complex, layered iris patterns, while AI eyes tend to be too uniform. The branch it sits on may also have unnatural geometry. [CURATOR: replace URL with Flux bird image from a stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with DALL-E 3 (subject to OpenAI ToS)
items.explanation: This AI-generated image of books on a shelf is a great test. Look at the book spines: any visible text will be garbled or nonsensical. The books may have impossible proportions — spines too thin for the page thickness, or covers that don't connect to the pages. Shadows between books may fall in inconsistent directions. [CURATOR: replace URL with DALL-E bookshelf/objects image from a stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Stable Diffusion XL (not copyrightable)
items.explanation: This AI-generated aurora scene is the hardest item in the set. Auroras are already surreal-looking in real life, so the usual 'too perfect' heuristic doesn't help. Look instead at the ground/foreground: are the trees or terrain too smooth? Do the stars look like uniform dots rather than varying brightness? Is there a reflection that doesn't quite match the sky? [CURATOR: replace URL with SDXL aurora image from Lexica or similar stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Midjourney v6 (subject to Midjourney ToS)
items.explanation: This AI-generated underwater scene with colorful coral is convincing because underwater photos are naturally blurry and distorted. Look for repeating coral patterns — AI often copies the same shape multiple times. Fish may have wrong fin counts or eyes on both sides of the head (visible at once). The overall color palette may be too uniformly vivid. [CURATOR: replace URL with Midjourney underwater image from a stable source]

items.attribution: AI-generated with Flux (not copyrightable)
items.explanation: This AI-generated macro insect image is tricky because macro photography already looks otherworldly. Look at the legs: insects have exactly 6 legs, and AI often adds or removes some. Check the compound eyes — real insect eyes have extremely regular hexagonal facets, while AI tends to make them blobby. Antennae may fork or merge in unnatural ways. [CURATOR: replace URL with Flux macro insect image from a stable source]
